channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TSC Alerts Teachers to Fake Salary Increment Notice Circulating Online
item.description: The Teachers Service Commission (TSC) has warned teachers across Kenya to disregard a fake notice circulating online that falsely claims a July 2026 salary increment. The forged document, dated July 1...

item.title: Sleep Strategies of Elite Athletes for Optimal Performance
item.description: The article explores the sleep routines and strategies of elite athletes Tom Dean and Alex Yee, highlighting their methods to ensure restorative sleep despite rigorous training schedules. Dean, a trip...

item.title: Iran launches retaliatory strikes against US allies in Middle East, targeting infrastructure
item.description: Iran has escalated tensions in the Middle East by launching retaliatory strikes against US allies, targeting critical infrastructure such as desalination plants, power facilities, and military bases i...

item.title: Codeine Seizures in Madhya Pradesh's Vindhya Region
item.description: In the Vindhya region of Madhya Pradesh, authorities have uncovered large quantities of illicit cough syrups containing codeine, a highly addictive substance. The drug trade has become rampant in area...

item.title: Mixx World Cup Campaign Rewards 80 Winners with Cash and Prizes
item.description: The Mixx Super App campaign, which encourages users to make financial payments through the app, has rewarded 80 participants with cash prizes and Hisense electronic appliances. During weekly draws, wi...

item.title: Kemi Badenoch's Conservative Rebranding and Ideological Shift
item.description: Kemi Badenoch, leader of the UK Conservative Party, has initiated a significant ideological realignment by rejecting 'net zero' environmental policies and the European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR...

item.title: Government revokes idle mining licenses to boost production and support small-scale miners
item.description: The Tanzanian government has intensified efforts to revoke mining licenses held by inactive investors, with Minerals Minister Antony Mavunde warning that such licenses will be reallocated to active mi...

item.title: Foreign Woman in Karachi's DHA Files FIR Against Suspected Rapist
item.description: A foreign woman residing in Karachi's Defence Housing Authority (DHA) has filed a first information report (FIR) against a suspect after allegedly being raped. The incident occurred on July 17, 2026, ...

item.title: Tragedy at King David Junior School Claims Lives of Students and Teacher
item.description: A memorial post has revealed the names of 21 pupils and one teacher from King David Junior School in Kampala who died in the Kapchorwa bus crash on 16 July 2026. The incident, which shocked Uganda and...

item.title: Mandela's Legacy: Lessons in Character and Leadership
item.description: The article reflects on Nelson Mandela's enduring influence through his character and values rather than academic credentials. It highlights how Mandela's decision to forgive his oppressors and priori...

item.title: Anthropic Expands AI Model Access with Usage Limits in Premium Plans
item.description: Anthropic, the AI research company, announced on July 20, 2026, that it will integrate its advanced Fable 5 model into the Max and Team Premium subscription plans, offering it at 50% of weekly usage l...

item.title: Natalie Imbruglia Reflects on Career, Fears, and Personal Traits in New Interview
item.description: Natalie Imbruglia, 51, shares candid insights about her life, career, and personal struggles in a recent interview. Born in Sydney to an Italian father and Australian mother, she rose to fame with her...

item.title: Elizabeth McGovern's 1980s Career and Iconic Looks
item.description: This article explores Elizabeth McGovern's rise to fame during the 1980s, highlighting her distinctive style and notable film roles. Born in 1961, McGovern became a prominent actress known for her nat...

item.title: U.S. Ambassador Warns of Tariffs Over Canadian Wildfire Smoke Impact
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Canada Pete Hoekstra has defended President Donald Trump’s threat to impose tariffs on Canada over smoke from wildfires, citing economic and health impacts on American cities like D...

item.title: China's AI Leadership and Global Governance Efforts

item.title: Debate Over FBI Director Kash Patel's Handling of ICE Shooting Investigations

item.description: Banyana Banyana coach Desiree Ellis has confirmed the squad for the Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon) and emphasized that team harmony will prevail, dispelling concerns about bonus disputes with ...

item.title: Ekiti State Achieves Record IGR of N2.7bn Through Tax Reforms and Compliance
item.description: The Ekiti State Government has reported a significant increase in its Internal Generated Revenue (IGR) for the month, reaching N2.7 billion without introducing new taxes. The achievement is attributed...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Leadership Transition and Policy Plans as Labour's New Prime Minister
item.description: The article details Andy Burnham's rapid rise to leadership within the Labour Party following Sir Keir Starmer's resignation. After years of political setbacks, Burnham's third attempt at leadership h...

item.title: India's private rocket Vikram-1 successfully places 6 payloads in low-Earth orbit
item.description: India's private space venture, Vikram Aerospace, has achieved a historic milestone by successfully launching its rocket Vikram-1 and placing six payloads into low-Earth orbit. The mission, which marks...
